scope_description: cross-platform-install feature (macOS + Windows no-clone install), both slices, 4 cells. User pre-authorized this review as part of an autonomous 3-task run, conditional on the feature being non-disruptive to existing behavior - confirmed via cell prohibitions verified byte-unchanged for Linux.

excluded.reason: belongs to the concurrent doctor-config-surface feature, not in scope for this review

head: 7af78f9830bcdd9424384ce82fe061f5618b465c

reviewers.trigger: external network calls (curl/Invoke-WebRequest downloads), CI job additions

findings.title: install.ps1 writes BOM-encoded config.json, breaking serde_json parsing on every Windows launch
findings.summary: install.ps1's Set-Content -Encoding utf8 emits a UTF-8 BOM on PowerShell 5.1; serde_json::from_str does not skip BOM, so the binary fails to parse config.json on every launch - the Windows service never starts.
findings.current_behavior: install.ps1:140 (as capped) wrote config.json via ($config | ConvertTo-Json) | Set-Content -Path $ConfigFile -Encoding utf8.
findings.impact: The headline Windows deliverable (a one-command installer) would be dead on arrival for every user on the default PowerShell 5.1 shell.
findings.scenario: User runs irm .../install.ps1 | iex; the Scheduled Task launches herdr-go --config config.json; the binary exits on a config parse error at column 1; -RestartCount 3 retries and fails identically.
findings.proposed_fix: Write BOM-free UTF-8 via [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText with UTF8Encoding($false).
findings.resolution: Fixed in cell cross-platform-install-5: config.json now written via [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText with a BOM-free UTF8Encoding($false). Delta re-reviewed and swept for the same defect class (no other file writes in install.ps1/install.sh use a BOM-prone encoding path).

findings.title: packaging/herdr-go.plist is shipped but never consumed - two unsynchronized sources of plist truth
findings.reviewer: architecture, test-coverage, code-quality (corroborated by 3 independent reviewers)
findings.summary: install.sh's Darwin branch writes its own inline plist heredoc; packaging/herdr-go.plist is a separate template copied into the release archive by release.yml but never substituted or consumed by install.sh. The template's own header comment incorrectly claims install.sh substitutes it.

evidence.related: packaging/herdr-go.plist header comment; release.yml Package step

findings.proposed_fix: Either make install.sh sed-substitute the shipped template, or drop the placeholder template and correct its comment to 'reference copy only, not consumed by install.sh'. Add a CI check asserting the two stay in sync or that the template is genuinely unused.

findings.title: Neither installer verifies integrity of the downloaded release archive
findings.summary: install.sh and install.ps1 both download the release archive over HTTPS with no checksum/signature verification. herdr-go's own release job publishes no SHA256SUMS artifact to check against (the only checksum in release.yml is for the unrelated upstream herdr smoke-test binary).
findings.proposed_fix: Publish a SHA256SUMS in the release job; have both installers compute-and-compare before installing.

findings.title: No CI regression coverage for install.sh or install.ps1 - not even a syntax/parse check
findings.summary: The macos-14 CI job runs only cargo fmt/clippy/test; it never invokes or even syntax-checks install.sh. install.ps1 has zero committed coverage anywhere (no PSScriptAnalyzer, no Pester, no parse check), despite pwsh already being available on the windows-server-2022 runner.

evidence.related: no shellcheck/bats/Pester/PSScriptAnalyzer config anywhere in the repo

findings.proposed_fix: Add a bash -n install.sh step to the macos-14 job; add a PowerShell parse/PSScriptAnalyzer step to the windows-server-2022 job. Both runners are already provisioned.

findings.title: base_config_dir/base_data_dir independently enumerate the same platform cfg-set - drift risk
findings.summary: Both functions repeat an identical 3-arm cfg ladder (windows/macos/other-unix). A future platform arm added to one but not the other would silently split config from data with no compile error.
findings.proposed_fix: Extract one native_base_dirs() helper returning the platform root(s); derive both functions from it.

findings.title: No timeout on either installer's download call - a stalled connection hangs the installer forever
findings.summary: install.sh's curl and install.ps1's Invoke-WebRequest both have unbounded timeouts. A dropped connection fails cleanly, but a stalled TCP connection (captive portal, half-open socket) hangs the one-liner install indefinitely.
findings.proposed_fix: Add --connect-timeout 20 --max-time 300 to curl; add -TimeoutSec 300 to Invoke-WebRequest.

findings.title: macOS upgrade tears down the working service and overwrites the binary before proving the replacement loads
findings.summary: On re-run/upgrade, install.sh's Darwin branch overwrites the live binary and bootouts the running service BEFORE bootstrap of the new plist is proven to succeed. If bootstrap/load fails, the machine ends with the service down and the old binary already gone - worse than the pre-install state, with no rollback.

evidence.lines: Darwin block: install -m 0755 ... then later launchctl bootout ... bootstrap ... || die

findings.proposed_fix: Bootstrap/verify the new binary loads before overwriting the live binary, or snapshot the old binary and restore it on any post-bootout failure.

findings.title: P3 cleanup batch: config JSON duplicated across installers; release.yml exact-match target string; expect() vs documented error; retry/redirect hardening
findings.summary: (1) Default config JSON is hand-duplicated in install.sh and install.ps1 - a schema change needs 3 edits. (2) release.yml's Package step matches the exact string aarch64-apple-darwin, not a *-apple-darwin pattern - will silently ship the wrong (systemd) service file into a future Intel-Mac archive once D11's Intel support lands. (3) native_macos_app_support's doc comment promises an actionable startup error but both callers .expect() it (matches pre-existing Windows pattern, so consistent but the comment overpromises). (4) No retry on transient download failures in either installer. (5) install.sh's curl pins the initial scheme (--proto '=https') but not the redirect hop (--proto-redir).

evidence.lines: see individual reviewer reports in docs/history/cross-platform-install/reports/validation-slice2-windows.md and the review session findings

findings.proposed_fix: Track as backlog cleanup items; none block current release.

findings.title: install.ps1 re-echoes the existing login token on every run, not just first creation
findings.summary: install.ps1's token-echo loop runs unconditionally whenever the token file exists, printing the secret into terminal scrollback on every re-install/upgrade. install.sh only prints on first creation. install.ps1's own comment claims parity with that contract but doesn't implement it.

evidence.related: install.sh: echo gated on first-creation block only

findings.proposed_fix: Gate the echo on the token file not existing before this run started (matching install.sh's contract).

uat.deliverable: config_dir()/data_dir() resolve to ~/Library/Application Support/herdr-go on macOS
macOS auto-start via LaunchAgent, no secret in the plist
uat.evidence_ref: 5-reviewer session confirmed no EnvironmentVariables/secret in packaging/herdr-go.plist or install.sh's generated plist

uat.deliverable: install.sh works on Darwin (arm64), --uninstall leaves state intact
uat.evidence_ref: code review (5 reviewers) + cell verification; no P1 found here

uat.deliverable: Windows PowerShell installer works, -Uninstall leaves state intact
uat.evidence_ref: P1 (BOM breaking config parse) found and fixed (commit ccd24e0); re-verified clean after fix

uat.item: D5 Windows Scheduled Task, no elevation
uat.deliverable: RunLevel Limited, logon trigger, no admin required
uat.evidence_ref: code review confirmed RunLevel on correct cmdlet (New-ScheduledTaskPrincipal)

uat.deliverable: macOS archive contains herdr-go.plist, not herdr-go.service
uat.evidence_ref: real release tag test v0.0.0-macos-smoke-test, downloaded and inspected archive contents directly

gate4.reason: 1 P1 found (install.ps1 BOM-encoded config.json breaking serde_json parsing on every Windows launch) and fixed at the root (commit ccd24e0); delta re-reviewed and swept for the same defect class (no other instance in install.ps1/install.sh). 8 P2/P3 findings recorded as non-blocking backlog. All 6 UAT items confirmed pass by the user. cargo test/clippy green at close.

decision.summary: Review session for cross-platform-install (macOS + Windows no-clone install, 5 cells) approved. 5-reviewer panel (code-quality, architecture, security, test-coverage, reliability) found and the team fixed 1 P1 before merge; both previously-caught pre-execution security findings (macOS plist secret injection, Windows token-file ACL inheritance) were independently re-confirmed fixed in the shipped code by the security reviewer. 8 non-blocking P2/P3 findings filed to backlog for follow-up.
